name: Ellett Law Offices, P.C.
description_short: Arizona-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7, 11, and 13 filings with flat-rate pricing and a 96% Chapter 13 confirmation rate since 1993.
description_long:
Ellett Law Offices, P.C. has operated as an Arizona bankruptcy law firm for over 30 years, founded by Ronald J. Ellett who brings more than 20 years of legal experience. The firm is located in the Phoenix area and has assisted thousands of individuals and businesses navigate financial difficulties through bankruptcy proceedings. Their longevity and established reputation position them as an experienced provider in Arizona's bankruptcy legal market.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy representation across three primary filing types: Chapter 7 (liquidation), Chapter 13 (reorganization with repayment plans), and Chapter 11 (business restructuring). They provide flat-rate pricing for Chapter 7 cases ranging from $1,695 to $2,295 depending on creditor count, plus $338 court filing fees. Chapter 13 cases feature a zero-down filing option where remaining legal fees are paid through the repayment plan. Additional services include lien stripping, foreclosure prevention, credit card debt relief, divorce-related bankruptcy matters, and exemption planning. The firm emphasizes asset protection for vehicles, homes, businesses, and retirement accounts.

Ellett Law distinguishes itself through documented performance metrics. Their Chapter 13 confirmation rate stands at 96% compared to 69% for other attorney-represented cases and only 9% for pro se (self-represented) filings, based on U.S. Bankruptcy Court data from Arizona (December 2016–April 2017). The firm offers free initial consultations, requires no office visits, and provides transparent pricing with itemized creditor-based fee structures. They accept zero-down Chapter 13 filings with repayment plan integration, reducing upfront financial barriers.

The firm's primary limitation is geographic scope—they serve Phoenix and neighboring Arizona areas only. Their published confirmation rate data, while strong, comes from a specific 5-month period (2016–2017) and includes the caveat that "results vary by case and cannot be guaranteed." Chapter 7 flat rates exclude additional fees for reaffirmation agreements ($150 each), Notice of Bankruptcy Filing ($85 each), and case-specific costs. Potential clients should verify current fee structures and confirmation rates, as bankruptcy outcomes depend heavily on individual financial circumstances and case complexity.
pros: 96% Chapter 13 confirmation rate versus 69% average for attorney-represented cases and 9% for pro se filers
Zero-down Chapter 13 filing available with remaining fees paid through repayment plan
Transparent flat-rate pricing for Chapter 7: $1,695–$2,295 depending on creditor count (9 or fewer through 21+)
Free initial consultation with no office visit required
30+ years of firm history (since 1993) with founding attorney having 20+ years legal experience
Specific asset protection focus: vehicles, homes, retirement accounts, and businesses
Multi-chapter expertise: Chapter 7, 11, and 13 plus ancillary services (lien stripping, foreclosure prevention)
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only Phoenix and neighboring Arizona areas
Chapter 7 flat rates exclude additional per-agreement fees ($150 reaffirmation, $85 per notice filing)
Confirmation rate data sourced from limited 5-month period (Dec 2016–Apr 2017) with explicit disclaimer that results vary and cannot be guaranteed
Some pricing restrictions apply; Chapter 7 fees must be paid in full before filing, unlike Chapter 13's payment plan option
best_for: Arizona residents (Phoenix area) filing Chapter 7 bankruptcy with 9–21 creditors seeking transparent flat-rate pricing
Chapter 13 filers who cannot afford upfront legal fees and need zero-down filing with plan integration
Homeowners or business owners seeking asset protection and lien stripping during bankruptcy proceedings
Individuals facing foreclosure who need both bankruptcy and property retention strategies
diagnosis: Ellett Law Offices is best suited for Phoenix-area residents seeking experienced, fee-transparent bankruptcy representation with a documented track record in Chapter 13 confirmation. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Arizona and the need to verify current fee structures and case-specific costs beyond published flat rates, as individual bankruptcy outcomes vary significantly.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing with flat-rate legal fees
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing with zero-down option and plan-based fee payment
Credit card debt relief through bankruptcy
Bankruptcy exemptions planning and asset protection
Notice of Bankruptcy Filing preparation ($85 per filing)
Representation throughout bankruptcy case confirmation and completion
